task.title: Create a Collection
task.shortdesc: Create a Postman collection to group related API requests and organize your testing and documentation workflow.

prereq.p: Before you create a collection, ensure that you are signed in to your Postman account and have a workspace open.

context.p: A collection is a container for API requests in Postman. Collections let you organize requests into folders, run them in sequence, and generate documentation automatically. Creating a well-structured collection is the first step for both API testing and API documentation in Postman.

step.cmd: Select the + icon next to the Collections heading, or select New from the top navigation bar and then select Collection.
step.stepresult: A new untitled collection appears in the sidebar with the name field active.

step.cmd: Type a name for the collection and press Enter.

info.p: Use a descriptive name that reflects the API or feature set the collection covers. For example: User Management API or Payment Gateway — v2.

step.cmd: Select the collection name to open the collection overview panel.
In the Description field, write an overview of the API in Markdown.

info.p: The collection description appears at the top of your published API documentation. Include the purpose of the API, the base URL, authentication requirements, and any important notes for API consumers.

step.cmd: To add a folder, hover over the collection name in the sidebar, select the ... (More Actions) icon, and then select Add folder.

info.p: Use folders to group requests by resource type or feature area. For example: Authentication, Users, and Orders.

step.cmd: Type a name for the folder, add a description, and press Enter.

result.p: The collection and folder structure appears in the sidebar. You can now add requests to the collection.

postreq.p: Add API requests to the collection. For instructions, see .
